description:
Goal
Make compiler2 internal runtime transport preserve the full recursive structure of direct-only callable captures so a direct callable may capture another direct callable without collapsing to flat ABI lanes or ValueRef recovery.

Problem being fixed
The current shared transport model introduced in fz-hwn.6 added RuntimeValueLayout::{Omitted,Value,TupleFields,DirectCallableCaptures}, but DirectCallableCaptures still stores only function identity plus flat capture_tys/capture_reprs. The same flattening exists one level earlier in DirectCallableWitness. That means compiler2 can represent only callable captures that are ordinary runtime values. When a direct-only lambda captures another direct-only callable, the compiler loses the fact that the capture is itself structured. One path sees zero ABI lanes and treats the capture as absent; another path falls back to ValueRef because no recursive callable layout exists. Native then reports the downstream contradiction, but the first wrong representation is artifact transport derivation.

Concrete repro
compiler2_native_program_jit_runs_enum_reduce_through_compiler2_codegen currently fails because bridge lambda FunctionId(164) captures reducer parameter FunctionId(65), which is itself a zero-capture direct-only callable. Semantic/runtime demand says the capture exists. The current flat witness/layout model derives FunctionId(164) input transport as ValueRef or zero flat capture lanes instead of preserving the nested direct-callable structure. Downstream consumers then disagree about whether lambda 164 has one semantic capture or zero.

Root cause
DirectCallableWitness and RuntimeValueLayout::DirectCallableCaptures describe captures by flat ABI lanes instead of recursive runtime value layouts. This is a data-model bug. Native is only the first consumer strict enough to detect it.

Design decision
Do not add another native-only internal callable layout. Replace the flat witness and flat direct-callable layout with one recursive runtime value layout that already serves all internal transport. RuntimeInputLayout should become a semantic-index wrapper around that value layout instead of duplicating a second flat transport enum. Local callable facts should describe producer structure, not pre-flattened ABI shape. Backend/native local environments should store structured recursive values, and flattening to ABI lanes should happen only when a concrete boundary layout requires it.

Required data-model change
The recursive transport model must let a direct callable carry:
- exact target identity
- a sequence of capture layouts
- where each capture layout is itself a runtime value layout

Tuple-field transport should also compose recursively so one layout family describes any internal boundary-carried value.

This recursive model must become the only authority for direct-only callable transport across:
- executable input layouts
- runtime return layouts
- delivered resume layouts
- local lambda/function-ref witness resolution
- backend interpreter binding and materialization
- native internal value binding, capture transport, and direct-call argument encoding

Algorithm
1. Replace DirectCallableWitness { capture_tys, capture_reprs } with local callable producer facts that preserve function identity plus captured ValueIds.
2. Replace flat RuntimeValueLayout::DirectCallableCaptures and flat RuntimeInputLayout::DirectCallableCaptures with a recursive RuntimeValueLayout::DirectCallable { function, captures: Vec<RuntimeValueLayout> } shape. RuntimeInputLayout should carry semantic_index plus one RuntimeValueLayout instead of repeating transport variants.
3. Teach runtime transport derivation to resolve recursive direct-callable layouts from settled callable witnesses and runtime demand. Witness resolution must descend through captured values instead of converting captured values into type/repr vectors.
4. Update backend interpreter local values so direct-callable values preserve nested captures structurally instead of extracting only a flat lane vector.
5. Update native local values and transport helpers so direct-only values carry recursive structure. Any flattening to entry lanes, call lanes, or continuation lanes must be derived from the recursive layout at the concrete boundary, not baked into the value itself.
6. Remove helper logic that re-derives capture_tys/capture_reprs from value types or lambda capture counts once the recursive model is in place.

Removal target
- Delete flat capture_tys/capture_reprs storage from DirectCallableWitness.
- Delete flat capture_tys/capture_reprs storage from RuntimeValueLayout::DirectCallableCaptures and RuntimeInputLayout::DirectCallableCaptures.
- Delete duplicated input-vs-value transport enums if the recursive value layout can serve both with a semantic-index wrapper.
- Delete native and backend helper logic that treat direct-callable captures as plain Vec<Var> / Vec<AnyValue> without structural information.
- Delete any fallback that recovers callable shape from types for direct-only transport.

Checks
- compiler2_native_program_jit_runs_enum_reduce_through_compiler2_codegen passes.
- Enum.reduce artifact/backend tests prove nested direct-only callable captures remain direct and preserve semantic capture count even when a nested callable contributes zero ABI lanes.
- Existing direct-only tests stay green: compiler2_abi_ready_keeps_direct_only_enum_reduce_out_of_callable_inventory, compiler2_abi_ready_passes_direct_callable_captures_without_a_heap_closure_lane, compiler2_runtime_demand_keeps_a_returned_direct_callable_out_of_first_class_inventory, compiler2_interp_runs_quicksort_from_backend_artifacts.
- --log-telemetry shows executable 164 transport preserving the nested callable capture structurally, with no first-class callable entry introduced solely to carry that capture.
title: Compiler2 runtime transport: preserve nested direct-callable captures recursively
